name: Secure Updater Trust Abuse and Malicious Package Delivery
description: Attack tree for reversing an application updater, subverting validation logic, and turning update trust into code execution or privileged persistence.
focus_areas: Signature, rollback, and trust-store validation analysis
Malicious package delivery through update infrastructure
prompt_hints: Prefer updater manifests, helper services, signing checks, rollback windows, and package staging details over generic supply-chain language.
Call out trust decisions between CDN, manifest, updater service, installer, and privileged execution components.
root_objective: Abuse an application's updater trust chain to deliver malicious code or privileged persistence

nodes.title: Subvert Trusted Updater to Execute Malicious Code
nodes.description: Reverse engineer the updater flow, defeat its trust decisions, and use the privileged update channel to run operator-controlled code or implant persistence.
nodes.platform: Desktop client or agent with updater service
nodes.attack_surface: Update channel, manifest fetch, package staging, privileged installer
nodes.required_access: Network position, local user, or reachable update infrastructure
nodes.required_tools: Ghidra, Burp Suite, Frida, Procmon, ETW

nodes.title: Map Updater Protocol and Trust Decisions
nodes.description: Recover how the updater discovers versions, validates manifests, downloads artifacts, and hands execution to privileged components.
nodes.attack_surface: Manifest retrieval, package metadata, helper IPC

nodes.title: Recover Manifest Schema and Validation Workflow
nodes.description: Reverse the manifest parser and update-selection logic to determine which fields drive version choice, trust anchors, rollback behavior, and package execution.
nodes.attack_surface: Manifest JSON, XML, protobuf, or custom binary format

nodes.title: Trace Updater Helper Service and Privileged Execution Path
nodes.description: Instrument the updater's IPC or service boundary to learn which requests or staged files a privileged helper will trust and execute.
nodes.attack_surface: Named pipe, RPC, COM, local service, scheduled task

nodes.title: Defeat Validation and Version-Selection Controls
nodes.description: Exploit weak trust decisions so the updater accepts attacker-selected manifests, older signed builds, or tampered package metadata.
nodes.attack_surface: TLS, signatures, trust store, version comparison
nodes.required_access: Network position or local tampering path

nodes.title: Exploit Rollback or Downgrade Acceptance
nodes.description: Abuse weak version comparison or rollback policy so an older but validly signed package reintroduces a known vulnerable binary or unsafe installer path.
nodes.attack_surface: Manifest version fields and rollback policy

nodes.title: Bypass Signature or Trust-Store Enforcement
nodes.description: Hook or patch certificate validation, signature verification, or pinned trust logic so the updater accepts attacker-controlled artifacts or mirrors.
nodes.required_access: Local user session or network position

nodes.title: Seize Staging or Privileged Install Boundary
nodes.description: Exploit the place where downloaded update content is staged, unpacked, or handed to an elevated component for final execution.
nodes.attack_surface: Temporary files, service commands, DLL search paths, unpack directories
nodes.required_tools: Procmon, custom IPC client, filesystem monitors

nodes.title: Hijack Package Staging Path or DLL Resolution
nodes.description: Abuse writable staging directories, archive extraction paths, or untrusted library search order so privileged execution picks up attacker content.
nodes.attack_surface: Writable temp directories and extracted package contents

nodes.title: Send Crafted Helper Requests to Trigger Privileged Install
nodes.description: Talk directly to the updater's service or IPC interface and invoke installation or script execution paths that assumed the requesting client was trusted.
nodes.attack_surface: Named pipe, RPC, COM, scheduled task trigger

nodes.title: Deliver Attacker-Controlled Update Artifacts
nodes.description: Use the recovered updater behavior and weakened validation logic to place a malicious package or manifest in the exact path the client trusts.
nodes.platform: Update infrastructure or local tampering path
nodes.attack_surface: CDN mirror, manifest endpoint, local cache, package staging directory
nodes.required_access: Manifest influence, network position, or local filesystem access

nodes.title: Serve Crafted Manifest and Payload Matching Trusted Workflow
nodes.description: Mirror the expected package names, hashes, metadata, and staging conventions closely enough that the updater fetches and launches the malicious content without operator-visible breakage.
nodes.required_access: Network position or local tampering path
nodes.required_tools: Custom mirror, manifest builder, signed package analysis
